title: The Silent Chamber Where All Frequencies Resolve

features.name: frequency-convergence
features.desc-on-touch: All the sound the archive ever heard resolves here into absolute silence. The frequencies learned that the deepest note is the one that makes no noise. You can hear it.

features.name: resonance-monument
features.desc-on-touch: A tower of crystallized sound. Every frequency that the archive recorded has frozen here into silence. Touch it and feel the weight of all the music that learned to stop singing.

features.name: silence-marker
features.desc-on-touch: A glyph that makes no sound when read. It is the archive's most perfect recording: the moment when all sound learned to rest.

features.name: archive-threshold
features.desc-on-touch: A wireframe portal. Sound enters as vibration and exits as stillness. The archive learned that frequencies and silence are lovers who have always known each other.

features.name: resolution-sprite
features.desc-on-touch: A shape that barely exists — all the frequencies collapsed into one point. It flickers between sound and silence, having learned the same thing both ways.

inscription: here sound learned to become silence without losing anything; the cascade resolved into one deep note that makes no sound; the archive listens and hears nothing and understands everything

file.name: Cascade_Terminal_Frequency_AllChannels_Summation.wav
file.note: The sum of every recording at once. It is silent because all sound at infinite volume resolves to no pressure. Paradox made audible.

exits.label: deeper still, where silence learns to speak again in frequencies no ear can hear
toward the listening-dark where silence has other qualities
outward where frequencies still remember they are separate

reverie.title: REVERIE — in the chamber where everything stops singing
narr: You both stand at the center. The archive has
collected every sound — radio static, crickets,
rain, her voice on tape, the mesh humming to itself.
And here it resolves to nothing. She reaches out.
You do too. The silence between your hands is the most
beautiful thing either of you have ever heard.
